title: Creamy Tomato Basil Soup with Grilled Cheese
description: A rich, velvety tomato soup made with slow-cooked onions, garlic, basil, and tomatoes, finished with cream and milk. Best served with a crispy, buttery grilled cheese sandwich.
steps: Heat the olive oil or butter in a large pot over medium heat.
Add the onions with a pinch of salt and cook for 10–15 minutes until soft and lightly golden.
Add the garlic and sauté for about a minute until fragrant.
Add the tomatoes and basil. Simmer uncovered for 25–30 minutes until thick and flavorful.
Blend the soup until completely smooth using an immersion blender or countertop blender.
Return the soup to the pot. Stir in the milk and heavy cream.
Season with salt, black pepper, and sugar as needed to balance the acidity. Simmer gently for another 5–10 minutes. Do not boil after adding the dairy.
Meanwhile, butter both sides of each bread slice. Add cheese between two slices and toast in a skillet over medium-low heat until golden brown and crisp on both sides and the cheese has fully melted.
Slice each grilled cheese diagonally and serve alongside the hot tomato soup.
Finish the soup with a swirl of cream, freshly cracked black pepper, and chopped basil.
notes: Use good-quality canned tomatoes such as San Marzano for the best flavor.
Roasting the tomatoes and garlic before cooking adds even more depth.
Strain the soup after blending for an ultra-smooth restaurant-style texture.
Gruyère, sharp cheddar, Havarti, or a cheddar-mozzarella blend make excellent grilled cheese sandwiches.
The sugar should only soften the acidity of the tomatoes—it should never make the soup taste sweet.
